title: tium atratum
side_effects: Tium atratum can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and dizziness due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged consumption can lead to kidney damage, liver failure, and even death in severe cases. It may also cause allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, itching, and respiratory problems. Ingesting even small amounts can cause these side effects, highlighting the importance of proper handling and usage of this plant.
